steps.title: Spot the vertex form — read $(h, k)$ directly
steps.content: $y = -16(x - 6.8)^2 + 740$ is vertex form $a(x-h)^2 + k$. Read off: vertex $= (6.8,\ 740)$. No algebra needed.

steps.title: Since $a = -16 < 0$, the vertex is the maximum
steps.content: The parabola opens downward, so the vertex gives the maximum height: 740 ft at $t = 6.8$ s. That matches choice B — B is correct.
